title: Cafe manager
summary: A leadership role responsible for daily Wake Cup operations, team coordination, service quality, and guest satisfaction.
experience: 2+ years in cafe or hospitality management
responsibilities: Coordinate daily cafe operations and service flow
Support, train, and supervise baristas and service staff
Handle guest feedback and resolve service issues
Monitor coffee quality, cleanliness, and team discipline
Plan small cafe events and seasonal offers
requirements: Experience in cafe, restaurant, or coffee shop management
Ability to work under pressure during busy shifts
Understanding of hospitality standards and guest care
benefits: Coffee training and staff discounts may be available
Supportive team and clear work processes
body: The cafe manager keeps Wake Cup organized from opening to closing.
This role connects guests, baristas, kitchen staff, and owners into one clear daily workflow.
A good manager improves both guest experience and team confidence by setting clear standards.
highlights: Maintain service quality during busy shifts

title: Barista
summary: A coffee-focused role responsible for preparing espresso drinks, tea, pastries, and friendly cafe service.
responsibilities: Prepare espresso, cappuccino, latte, tea, and seasonal drinks
Serve pastries and simple cafe items
Keep the coffee station clean and organized
Support takeaway and dine-in coffee orders
Maintain friendly communication with guests
requirements: Interest in specialty coffee and guest service
Accuracy with recipes and drink presentation
Cleanliness and attention to station setup
Ability to work morning or daytime shifts
benefits: Growth into senior barista or cafe supervisor
body: The barista creates small daily moments for guests through coffee, tea, pastries, and friendly service.
This role requires attention to taste, presentation, cleanliness, and timing.
It is a good fit for someone who likes coffee culture and wants to grow in cafe service.

title: Pastry assistant
summary: A kitchen support role focused on pastries, dessert preparation, packing, and clean work areas.
responsibilities: Help prepare pastries, desserts, and cafe items
Pack takeaway pastry boxes and small orders
Keep kitchen tools and work areas clean
Support the barista team during busy service
Follow hygiene and food safety rules
requirements: Interest in pastry or cafe kitchen work
Ability to follow recipes and instructions
benefits: Good role for cafe kitchen growth
Possible growth into baker or cook
body: The pastry assistant helps Wake Cup keep fresh sweets ready for coffee breaks and takeaway orders.
This role is practical, detail-oriented, and important for the daily cafe rhythm.
Reliability, cleanliness, and readiness to learn matter most.

title: Service assistant
summary: A guest-facing role focused on welcoming guests, serving coffee and pastries, and keeping tables ready.
responsibilities: Welcome guests and help them choose from the menu
Serve coffee, pastries, and simple cafe items
Keep tables clean and ready for the next guests
Support takeaway orders when needed
Communicate guest requests to baristas and kitchen staff
requirements: Ability to work on feet during active shifts
Attention to orders, timing, and table details
Teamwork with barista and kitchen staff
benefits: Flexible shift options may be available
Growth into senior service or shift supervisor
body: The service assistant supports the main guest experience at Wake Cup.
Good service depends on timing, attention, polite communication, and teamwork.
This role is suitable for active people who enjoy working with guests.
highlights: Work closely with barista and kitchen teams
